changelog: Architectural fix for Reset to Default, replacing v15's patch: the SSH addon no longer turns off around a reset cycle at all. Every bug found in this feature since v9 (QR race, self-kill ordering, stray-trigger-on-restart, concurrency race with the maintenance branch, and v15's crash-on-blind-start) was some version of 'something went wrong around starting/stopping the addon' -- the addon now stays running permanently (boot: auto) with a persistent, random, per-device admin password (generated once, stored in /config/.ssh_admin_password, survives every Reset to Default on that unit -- NOT a fixed password shared across the fleet). shell_command.dev_reset is back down to just touching the trigger file; the watcher is already there to pick it up. reset.sh (golden-image prep) clears the password file so every unit cloned from a master image mints its own fresh one on first boot.
warnings: HA will restart automatically after this update to load the new shell_command definition.

SSH access behavior has changed: the Advanced SSH & Web Terminal addon will now stay running permanently instead of being off except during active operations, using a persistent per-device password (visible in /config/.ssh_admin_password) rather than being locked out after every Reset to Default.
